parts.title: Transcript
parts.annotated_transcript_html:
Speaker 1: This recording is copyright. You will hear a podcast on behalf of a company called Eventstars, which arranges staffing for music festivals. You now have some time to look at the questions on your screen. Now answer the questions on your screen.

Speaker 1: The good news is we still have openings at various festivals, which I'd like to tell you about, starting with Clasmurfest. We need people for our meet and greet team to welcome new arrivals to the festival and direct them to whichever of the camping zones they've got a ticket for and will be based in throughout the festival.

Speaker 1: And then, at Bigtime, we still have vacancies for the site clear-up during the three days once the festival is over, with generous hourly rates of pay, as well as discarded tickets and food containers, even things like tents get left behind, and it all needs to be disposed of.

Speaker 1: There's been a lot of interest in the brand-new Blestival this year. As well as featuring comedy, celebrity chefs, and some great bands, one of Blestival's key aims is to raise funds for much-needed wildlife conservation projects in developing countries. And for this, we need staff to take donations from the festival-goers. And the busiest times for this are when people are arriving at the start and leaving the site on the final day.

Speaker 1: And finally, we have a few openings at Smash, the celebration of rock music. The organizers have decided that there are now going to be at-the-gate ticket sales, and so we're looking for people for that, preferably with experience of handling contactless payments.

Speaker 1: Do please view our website and just follow the links if you'd like to apply. We're offering excellent working conditions, and believe me, the work is great fun.
